meta.title: Epistemic Expansion & AI
meta.subtitle: Starting from Cherny's insight — "you have to understand the layer under the layer at which you work" — this map clusters the key concepts around the human practitioner.
meta.note: Click or hover on nodes to explore connections. Drag nodes to reposition. Press D to dump positions to console, S to export SVG, P to export PNG.

foundation.label: What Must Reside in the Person

nodes.label:
The Human
Practitioner
nodes.description: The knowledge worker operating in AI-augmented environments. Central question: what must reside in the person vs. what can safely reside in the tool?

nodes.label:
Extended
Cognition
nodes.description: AI as cognitive prosthesis. Amplified by generative AI — the user's reach expands into unfamiliar domains. (Clark & Chalmers, 1998)

nodes.label:
Enacted
Cognition
nodes.description: Knowing through doing. Practice-based understanding that builds retrieval structures integrated with the task. What AI shortcuts bypass. (4E framework)

nodes.label:
Embodied
Cognition
nodes.description: The 'feels wrong' intuition. Expert pattern recognition that operates below conscious reasoning. Degrades with fatigue and disuse. (Dreyfus; 4E framework)

nodes.label:
Embedded
Cognition
nodes.description: Cognition situated in social/material context. Code reviews, team expertise, institutional memory. Stressed when people act outside their epistemic niche.

nodes.label:
Hollowed
Mind
nodes.description: Long-term dependency state where capacity for deep reasoning diminishes. The 'Sovereignty Trap' — choosing the path of least cognitive resistance. (Klein & Klein, 2025)

nodes.label:
Falling Asleep
at the Wheel
nodes.description: High-quality AI produces worse human performance. Users disengage, stop checking, lose vigilance. Better AI → lazier humans. (Dell'Acqua, 2022)

nodes.label:
Silent Domain
Shift
nodes.description: AI produces equally fluent output whether the problem is Complicated or Complex. No alarm sounds. The user who lacks enacted knowledge can't detect the transition.

nodes.label:
Complicated
Domain
nodes.description: Known unknowns. Expert analysis can determine the right answer. AI-extended cognition works well here — pattern-matching established practice. (Cynefin: Sense-Analyze-Respond)

nodes.label:
Complex
Domain
nodes.description: Unknown unknowns. Cause and effect only visible in retrospect. Requires probe-sense-respond. Where AI-extended-but-not-enacted cognition fails silently. (Cynefin)

nodes.label:
Jagged
Frontier
nodes.description: AI capabilities are unevenly distributed across tasks. The boundary is irregular and task-specific, not a clean line. Users must map it through experience. (Dell'Acqua et al., 2023)

nodes.label:
The Layer
Underneath
nodes.description: "You really have to understand the layer under the layer at which you work." The foundational knowledge that enables diagnosis when the working layer breaks. (Boris Cherny, Lenny's Podcast)

nodes.label:
Diagnostic
Capability
nodes.description: The ability to switch modes: from operating through AI to reasoning about what went wrong. Itself a Complex-domain activity — recursive: you need the skill to recognize you need the skill.

nodes.label:
Bainbridge's
Irony
nodes.description: Automation removes the practice needed to maintain skills required when automation fails. With AI: skills may never develop in the first place, and the need for them becomes invisible. (Bainbridge, 1983)

nodes.label:
Task
Expansion
nodes.description: Workers step into responsibilities beyond their domain. PM writes code, researcher does engineering. AI makes unfamiliar tasks feel accessible. (HBR study, 2025)

nodes.label:
Blurred
Boundaries
nodes.description: Work spills into breaks, evenings, early mornings. Prompting AI feels like chatting, not working. Downtime no longer provides recovery. (HBR study, 2025)
